generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor providing raw point cloud data for environmental perception in the autonomous vehicle system. Used for real-time spatial mapping and object detection."
    DTDFVocab:producedData RawLiDARPointCloudData, ProcessedLiDARObjectTracks
]

instance IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel Deepsense D435i depth camera capturing depth information and RGB images for enhanced environmental perception and sensor fusion with LiDAR data."
    DTDFVocab:producedData RawDepthCameraData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Onboard sensors capturing vehicle dynamics including motor, steering, servo actuation states, speed, and trajectory for real-time monitoring and control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

instance ActuationFeedbackSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensors monitoring motor, steering, and servo actuation feedback for closed-loop control and system validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ActuationFeedbackData
]

instance ACCRelevantDataSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensors providing Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) relevant data including projected driving paths, object tracks, and vehicle state information for real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ACCRelevantData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance RawLiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw point cloud data from Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR transmitted via Wi-Fi (UDP) for real-time processing."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance ProcessedLiDARObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks and environmental representations generated by Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking from raw LiDAR data."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance RawDepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw depth and RGB data from Intel Deepsense D435i Depth Camera transmitted for sensor fusion and environmental modeling."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Vehicle dynamics data including motor, steering, servo actuation states, speed, and trajectory transmitted for real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ActuationFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Actuation feedback data from motor, steering, and servo systems transmitted as part of the Actuation Functional Element."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ActuationFeedbackSensor
]

instance ACCRelevantData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) data including projected driving paths, object tracks, and vehicle state information for real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ACCRelevantDataSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric/Behavioral model implemented via DBSCAN for raw LiDAR data segmentation and Kalman filter for trajectory estimation, enabling real-time spatial and dynamic representation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateAggregationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model aggregating and formatting vehicle state data for downstream use in visualization and control."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance RawLiDARPointCloudDataModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw point cloud data from LiDAR sensors before preprocessing."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RawLiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingEnabler
]

instance ProcessedLiDARObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks and environmental representations generated from raw LiDAR data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedLiDARObjectTracks
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingEnabler, Unity3DVisualizationEnabler
]

instance RawDepthCameraDataModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw depth and RGB data from the Intel Deepsense D435i Depth Camera."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RawDepthCameraData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SensorFusionEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateDataModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated vehicle dynamics data including motor, steering, servo actuation states, speed, and trajectory."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler, ActuationControlEnabler
]

instance ActuationFeedbackDataModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed actuation feedback data for closed-loop control and system validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuationFeedbackData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuationControlEnabler
]

instance ACCRelevantDataModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed ACC data including projected driving paths, object tracks, and vehicle state information."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ACCRelevantData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AdaptiveCruiseControlEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component implementing DBSCAN for LiDAR data segmentation and Kalman filter for trajectory estimation, enabling real-time spatial and dynamic representation."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeEnvironmentalMappingService, Unity3DVisualizationService
]

instance DataPreProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional element filtering and structuring raw LiDAR data into actionable object tracks and environmental representations."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTranslationService, RealTimeDataMonitoringService
]

instance ActuationControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional element converting high-level commands into low-level control signals for physical actuators via OPC UA or equivalent protocols."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementAndControlService, AdaptiveCruiseControlService
]

instance SensorFusionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler combining LiDAR and depth camera data for enhanced environmental perception and sensor fusion."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeEnvironmentalMappingService
]

instance Unity3DVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unity-based immersive simulation platform for 3D visualization of LiDAR point clouds and vehicle trajectories."
    DTDFVocab:enables Unity3DVisualizationService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component serializing data, aligning message types, and performing unit conversions for consistency across the system."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTranslationService
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler optimizing Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) and executing actuator commands through ROS2-based control loops."
    DTDFVocab:enables AdaptiveCruiseControlService
]

instance ROS2NetworkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 network enabling modular and interoperable data exchange between functional elements like Data Pre-Processing FE, Actuation FE, and Controlling FE."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementAndControlService
]

instance WiFiUDPTransportEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi with UDP transport layer for low-latency, high-throughput inter-entity communication."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTranslationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeEnvironmentalMappingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing continuous collection and aggregation of sensor data (LiDAR, depth camera) via edge computing for system health and environmental state tracking."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnvironmentalStateInsight
]

instance Unity3DVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service offering immersive 3D simulation of the autonomous vehicle's operational environment, including LiDAR point clouds and trajectory prediction."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance RealTimeDataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling continuous monitoring of sensor data and system health for real-time operational adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemHealthInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementAndControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing remote management and control of the autonomous vehicle through ROS2-based control loops."
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteControlAction
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service optimizing Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) and executing actuator commands for physical-digital synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:provides CruiseControlOptimizationAction
]

instance DataTranslationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service serializing data, aligning message types, and performing unit conversions for cross-system consistency."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataConsistencyInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance EnvironmentalStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing real-time environmental state including object tracks, spatial mapping, and dynamic representations for operational decision-making."
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight offering 3D visualization of LiDAR point clouds, vehicle trajectories, and operational environment for monitoring and analysis."
]

instance SystemHealthInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing real-time system health monitoring based on sensor data aggregation and edge computing analysis."
]

instance RemoteControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action enabling remote management and control of the autonomous vehicle's actuators and systems."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance CruiseControlOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action optimizing Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) and executing actuator commands for physical-digital synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DataConsistencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight ensuring data consistency across the system through serialization, message alignment, and unit conversion."
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance VedderElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electronic speed controller regulating motor speed based on voltage commands generated by the Actuation Functional Element."
]

instance VehicleMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor executing mechanical motion as directed by the speed controller, with actuation ranges dependent on voltage-input specifications."
]

instance SteeringServo : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Servo providing directional control for the autonomous vehicle, interfacing with the Actuation Functional Element to translate steering commands into physical adjustments."
]

// C1: Components
instance PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Software component implementing DBSCAN for raw LiDAR data segmentation and Kalman filter for trajectory estimation."
]

instance DataPreProcessingComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Functional element filtering and structuring raw data into actionable representations for downstream processing."
]

instance ActuationControlComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Functional element converting high-level commands into low-level control signals for physical actuators."
]

instance DataTranslationComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Component serializing data, aligning message types, and performing unit conversions for cross-system consistency."
]

instance Unity3DVisualizationComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Unity-based platform for immersive 3D visualization of LiDAR data and vehicle trajectories."
]

// C10: Additional Models

    base:desc "Kalman filter model for trajectory estimation and dynamic representation of objects in the environment."

]

instance DBSCANClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [

]

// C11: Additional Enablers
instance NVIDIAJetpack50Enabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetpack 5.0 enabling edge AI acceleration and computation offloading between embedded (Jetson) and cloud resources."

]

instance MATLABSimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink for ACC system modeling and co-simulation with ROS2."

]

// C6: Additional Services

]

    base:desc "Insight enabling data collection, storage, and report generation for operational analysis and decision support."
]

    base:desc "Component managing collected data storage and traceability for reporting and analysis."
]

]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in this Digital Twin System (DTS) involves control commands generated by the **Digital Twin Entity** (e.g., virtual cockpit) and transmitted to the **Physical Twin Entity** (e.g., vehicle actuators) via the **Actuation FE**. The primary command types include:
- **ACC (Adaptive Cruise Control) Commands**: Enable/disable ACC, modify target speed, or trigger braking.
- **Motor/Steering/Servo Actuation Commands**: Direct adjustments to vehicle dynamics (e.g., throttle, steering angle, servo positions).

**Control Message Formats**:
- Commands are serialized and aligned by the **Data Translation FE**, ensuring compatibility with the **Actuation FE** and physical actuators. The exact message format is not specified, but it adheres to ROS2 message standards for internal communication.

**Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**:
- Not explicitly detailed in the document. The **Data Translation FE** likely performs basic validation (e.g., unit checks, range validation), but no authentication or cryptographic mechanisms are mentioned.

**Feedback Systems**:
- The **Actuation FE** provides feedback to the Digital Twin via sensor data loops (e.g., motor/steering state updates). This closed-loop system ensures the Digital Twin remains synchronized with the Physical Twin’s state.

**Conflict Resolution**:
- Not explicitly detailed. The system may rely on **priority-based arbitration** (e.g., safety-critical commands overriding non-critical ones), but this is inferred rather than stated.

**Command Execution Confirmation**:
- Implicit in the feedback loop; the Digital Twin monitors actuator responses (e.g., via **Vehicle State** sensors) to confirm command execution. No explicit acknowledgment protocol is described.

**Technical Constraints**:
- Commands must adhere to real-time constraints (e.g., <100ms latency for ACC adjustments) to ensure vehicle safety and responsiveness.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms time synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to hardware limitations. This temporal granularity is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter clock synchronization (e.g., sub-100 ms) is mandated by many standards. The system does not explicitly specify distinct time scales for different services but implies real-time constraints for sensor-actuator loops and user-facing components (e.g., 3D visualization and control panel) due to the ROS2-based architecture. Latency requirements are implicitly tied to the **ROS2 node execution model**, where inter-node communication (e.g., between *Physical Twin Entity* and *Digital Twin Entity*) must respect the 100 ms synchronization window to maintain consistency. No further granularity (e.g., microsecond-level precision) is documented beyond this hardware-enforced bound."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS orchestrates its constellation through a **ROS2-based microservice architecture**, where each functional entity (*Physical Twin Entity*, *Digital Twin Entity*, *User Entity*) is implemented as an **independent ROS2 package** containing one or more nodes. This design aligns with the **ISO 23247 RA** but introduces deviations: the *Physical Twin Entity* hosts general automotive functionality (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*) beyond resource-specific operations, altering ownership and engineering processes. **Resource management** is implicit via hardware partitioning (e.g., sensors/actuators in *Physical Twin Entity*), while **system-wide coordination** relies on ROS2’s **topic-based publish-subscribe model** and **service calls**, constrained by the 100 ms synchronization requirement. The architecture lacks a centralized orchestrator; instead, **entity-specific ROS2 nodes** manage intra-entity workflows (e.g., data pre-processing in *Physical Twin Entity*), with inter-entity communication mediated by ROS2’s distributed middleware. No dynamic reconfiguration or federated twin instances are documented."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is designed to provide a **real-time, high-fidelity computational reflection** of the **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**, ensuring synchronization between digital and physical states. Validation and verification methodologies are not explicitly described in the document, though the **User Entity** is tasked with monitoring data and interacting with the DTE. The **Data Collection and Device Control Entity** ensures data integrity by interfacing with sensors and actuators, but no **uncertainty quantification, error correction mechanisms, or quality assurance processes** are specified. The system’s safety-critical nature implies implicit validation requirements, but no explicit **model fidelity requirements** or **FE-level validation logic** (e.g., actuator command validation) are detailed."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin System (DTS) Technical Connection** infrastructure is structured into three entities (**Physical Twin**, **Digital Twin**, **User**) with distinct communication layers:

1. **Internal Communication (ROS2 Network)**:
   - **Protocol**: **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)**, a middleware framework for real-time communication between software components (e.g., **Data Pre-Processing FE**, **Actuation FE**, **Controlling FE**).
   - **Topology**: Pub-sub architecture where data streams (e.g., LiDAR, vehicle state) are published as topics and subscribed by relevant nodes.
   - **Latency Requirements**: Critical for real-time applications (e.g., ACC), with implied sub-100ms latency for control loops.

2. **Inter-Entity Communication (Wi-Fi Network)**:
   - **Protocol**: **Wi-Fi** (transport layer protocol unspecified; document cuts off at 'using U', likely UDP or TCP).
   - **Topology**: Point-to-point or mesh network connecting the **Physical Twin Entity** (vehicle sensors/actuators) to the **Digital Twin Entity** (virtual cockpit).
   - **Bandwidth Requirements**: High for LiDAR data streams (e.g., point cloud clustering) and low-latency control signals (e.g., ACC commands). Exact bandwidth not specified but inferred to be prioritized for real-time data.

3. **Reliability and Redundancy**:
   - **ROS2 QoS Policies**: Likely employed for reliability (e.g., best-effort or reliable QoS profiles), but not explicitly detailed in the document.
   - **Redundancy**: Not mentioned; no backup communication paths or failover mechanisms are described.

4. **Security Protocols**:
   - Not explicitly detailed. The **Data Translation FE** may include basic data integrity checks, but no encryption (e.g., WPA3) or authentication protocols are mentioned.

**Network Architecture**:
- **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts sensors (LiDAR, motor/steering/servo) and actuators.
- **Digital Twin Entity**: Runs visualization, control, and reporting services (e.g., **3D Visualization FE**, **Controlling FE**).
- **User Entity**: Interfaces via **User Interface FE** (e.g., virtual cockpit displays).

**Data Flow Propagation**:
- As shown in **Fig. 2**, sensor data (e.g., LiDAR, vehicle state) flows from the Physical Twin to the Digital Twin via **Data Pre-Processing FE** → **Data Translation FE** → **Reporting FE**/**Presentation FE**.
- Control commands flow from the Digital Twin to the Physical Twin via **Controlling FE** → **Actuation FE**.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin System (DTS) Hosting and Deployment** infrastructure is designed for real-time vehicle monitoring and control, with a focus on edge computing for low-latency operations. Key details include:

1. **Hosting Platforms**:
   - **Edge Deployment**: Primary hosting occurs on embedded hardware within the vehicle (e.g., **NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano** for the Physical Twin Entity), enabling real-time sensor data processing and actuation.
   - **Cloud/Off-Premise**: Non-critical computations (e.g., **ACC Controlling FE**) can be offloaded to the cloud or remote servers, as implied by the mention of 'e.g., in the cloud' for scalability. Specific cloud providers or services are not detailed.

2. **Computational Requirements**:
   - **Data Pre-Processing FE**: Requires high-performance processing for **Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking** (e.g., GPU acceleration on Jetson Orin Nano).
   - **Data Translation FE**: Acts as a central hub for serialization and unit conversion, with moderate computational demands.
   - **Controlling FE**: Demands low-latency processing for ACC logic (e.g., path planning, object avoidance).

3. **Storage Requirements**:
   - **SQL Database**: Used for structured data storage (e.g., vehicle state logs, LiDAR tracks) via the **Reporting FE** and **Data Storage FE**.
   - **Volatile Memory**: Likely used for real-time data buffers (e.g., ROS2 topic queues).

4. **Scalability Mechanisms**:
   - **Modular Design**: Services (e.g., **Data Collecting FE**, **Presentation FE**) are decoupled, allowing independent scaling.
   - **Offloading**: Non-real-time tasks (e.g., reporting, visualization) can be offloaded to cloud or edge nodes to reduce local computational load.
   - **Bottlenecks**: The **Data Translation FE** may act as a bottleneck due to its central role in data serialization/deserialization, but this is inferred rather than explicitly stated.

5. **Deployment Models Supported**:
   - **Hybrid Edge-Cloud**: Implied by the mention of cloud offloading for non-critical tasks, though specific orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes) is not detailed.
   - **On-Premise/Embedded**: Primary deployment is on-vehicle (e.g., Jetson Orin Nano) for real-time operations.

6. **Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
   - **Redundancy**: Not explicitly detailed; no mention of backup systems or failover nodes.
   - **Real-Time Constraints**: Critical for safety (e.g., <100ms latency for ACC commands), but no specific reliability metrics (e.g., uptime SLA) are provided.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 – Digital twin framework for manufacturing**, specifically **Part 2: Reference Architecture**, which defines:
  - An **entity-based architecture** with standardized roles for **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)**, **Data Collection and Device Control Entities**, **Digital Twin Entities**, and **User Entities**.
  - **Interoperability standards** via **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** for data exchange between physical and digital components.
  - **Compliance frameworks** ensuring alignment with manufacturing DT best practices, though no explicit **certification requirements** or **additional industry standards** (e.g., IEC 62443, NIST) are mentioned.
  The standard emphasizes **modularity** and **functional decomposition** but does not specify **formal certification processes** or **third-party validation** beyond ISO 23247 adherence."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC)** systems, as referenced in **ISO 15622:2018**, adheres to **functional safety requirements** for road vehicles, though no explicit mention of **ISO 26262** or **Automotive Safety Integrity Levels (ASIL)** is provided. The system is designed as a **safety-critical** application, requiring robust **performance testing and validation procedures** as outlined in the standard. **3GPP TS 22.185 (Release 15)** specifies **low end-to-end latency** as a critical requirement for **V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services**, ensuring real-time communication for safety-critical operations. The **model-driven architecture** for interactive digital twin cockpits (as described in [37]) emphasizes **real-time synchronization** and **consistent state representation**, which inherently supports safety-critical operations. No explicit cybersecurity protocols (e.g., intrusion detection, secure boot, or encryption) are detailed in the provided documents."
]
